questions.question: Which physical quantity is measured by an ammeter?
questions.explanation: An ammeter measures the flow of electric current in a circuit.

questions.question: The SI unit of force is:
questions.explanation: Force is measured in newton according to the SI system.

questions.question: Which lens is used to correct myopia?
questions.explanation: Concave lenses diverge light to correct short-sightedness.

questions.question: What is the acceleration due to gravity on Earth (approximate)?
questions.explanation: Standard gravitational acceleration is about 9.8 m/s².

questions.question: Which wave does not require a medium to travel?
questions.explanation: Light is an electromagnetic wave and can travel in vacuum.

questions.question: Which gas is produced when zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid?
questions.explanation: Zinc and hydrochloric acid produce hydrogen gas.

questions.question: The pH value of a neutral solution is:
questions.explanation: A neutral solution has equal H⁺ and OH⁻ ions giving pH 7.

questions.question: Which substance is used as a catalyst in the contact process?
questions.explanation: Vanadium pentoxide is used in sulfuric acid manufacture.

questions.question: What is the chemical formula of calcium carbonate?
questions.explanation: Calcium carbonate consists of Ca²⁺ and CO₃²⁻ ions.

questions.question: Which element is essential for rusting of iron?
questions.explanation: Rusting requires oxygen and moisture.

questions.question: The smallest unit of life is:
questions.explanation: Cells are the basic structural and functional units of life.

questions.question: Which organelle is known as the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce energy through respiration.

questions.question: Photosynthesis mainly occurs in which part of a plant?
questions.explanation: Leaves contain chlorophyll for photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which pigment is responsible for photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll absorbs light energy for photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which gas is released during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is released as a by-product of photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which blood cells help in clotting?
questions.explanation: Platelets help stop bleeding by clot formation.

questions.question: The normal body temperature of a human is:
questions.explanation: Normal human body temperature is about 37°C.

questions.question: Which organ filters blood in the human body?
questions.explanation: Kidneys filter waste from blood to form urine.

questions.question: Which vitamin is produced in the skin by sunlight?
questions.explanation: Sunlight helps synthesize vitamin D in skin.

questions.question: Which disease is caused by deficiency of iodine?
questions.explanation: Iodine deficiency causes enlargement of thyroid gland.

questions.question: What type of joint is present in the elbow?
questions.explanation: Elbow allows movement in one plane like a hinge.

questions.question: Which organ controls all activities of the body?
questions.explanation: The brain is the main control center of the body.

questions.question: What is the main function of red blood cells?
questions.explanation: RBCs carry oxygen using hemoglobin.

questions.question: Which hormone regulates blood sugar level?
questions.explanation: Insulin controls glucose level in blood.

questions.question: Which gas is mainly responsible for global warming?
questions.explanation: CO₂ traps heat causing greenhouse effect.

questions.question: Which process converts liquid into gas?
questions.explanation: Evaporation changes liquid into vapor.

questions.question: Which instrument measures atmospheric pressure?
questions.explanation: Barometer is used to measure air pressure.

questions.question: Which part of the ear helps in balancing the body?
questions.explanation: Semicircular canals maintain balance.

questions.question: Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
questions.explanation: Mercury remains liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Which acid is present in vinegar?
questions.explanation: Vinegar contains acetic acid.

questions.question: Which unit is used to measure electric power?
questions.explanation: Electric power is measured in watt.

questions.question: Which process is used by plants to lose excess water?
questions.explanation: Transpiration removes excess water through leaves.

questions.question: Which vitamin helps in blood clotting?
questions.explanation: Vitamin K is essential for clotting factors.

questions.question: Which organ stores bile?
questions.explanation: Gall bladder stores bile produced by liver.

questions.question: Which substance speeds up a chemical reaction without being used?
questions.explanation: Catalysts increase reaction rate without consumption.

questions.question: Which force keeps planets in their orbits?
questions.explanation: Gravitational force provides centripetal force.

questions.question: Which part of the plant conducts water?
questions.explanation: Xylem transports water and minerals.

questions.question: Which organ produces insulin?
questions.explanation: Pancreas secretes insulin hormone.

questions.question: Which gas is essential for respiration?
questions.explanation: Oxygen is required to release energy from food.

questions.question: Which phenomenon explains splitting of white light?
questions.explanation: Dispersion separates white light into colors.

questions.question: Which nutrient is the main source of energy?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates provide quick energy.

questions.question: Which type of energy is stored in food?
questions.explanation: Food stores energy in chemical bonds.

questions.question: Which part of the flower develops into fruit?
questions.explanation: Ovary enlarges to form fruit after fertilization.

questions.question: Which blood group is known as universal donor?
questions.explanation: O group blood can be donated to all groups.

questions.question: Which instrument is used to see distant objects clearly?
questions.explanation: Telescope is used for viewing distant objects.

questions.question: Which process produces new plants from seeds?
questions.explanation: Germination is the growth of seed into plant.

questions.question: Which metal is used for making electric wires?
questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Which organ helps in breathing?
questions.explanation: Lungs exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Which part of the brain controls balance?
questions.explanation: Cerebellum coordinates movement and balance.

questions.question: Which chemical is used to kill germs in drinking water?
questions.explanation: Chlorine disinfects drinking water.

questions.question: Which process breaks down food into simpler substances?
questions.explanation: Digestion converts food into absorbable form.

questions.question: Which part of the plant absorbs water from soil?
questions.explanation: Roots absorb water and minerals.

questions.question: Which substance turns blue litmus red?
questions.explanation: Acids change blue litmus to red.

questions.question: Which type of mirror forms a real image?
questions.explanation: Concave mirror can form real images.

questions.question: Which process is responsible for breathing in humans?
questions.explanation: Respiration involves breathing and energy release.

questions.question: Which vitamin prevents night blindness?
questions.explanation: Vitamin A maintains healthy vision.

questions.question: Which energy transformation occurs in an electric bulb?
questions.explanation: Bulb converts electrical energy into heat and light.

questions.question: Which part of blood fights disease?
questions.explanation: White blood cells defend against pathogens.

questions.question: Which force opposes motion between two surfaces?
questions.explanation: Friction resists relative motion of surfaces.

questions.question: Which gas is used in fire extinguishers?
questions.explanation: CO₂ does not support combustion and smothers fire.
